title: [PROJECT] Foundation & Data Infrastructure
description: Set up Next.js 14+ App Router project with SQLite database, TypeScript types, and core utilities

title: Initialize Next.js 14.2+ App Router with shadcn/ui, dark-mode fintech theme, and landing page
description: 1. Run `npx create-next-app@latest cross-border-tax --typescript --tailwind --app --eslint --src-dir=false --import-alias='@/*'` (select 'No' for src directory, 'Yes' for App Router, TypeScript, ESLint, Tailwind). 2. Install shadcn/ui: `cd cross-border-tax && npx shadcn-ui@latest init` (select: New York style, Slate base color, CSS variables=yes, RSC=yes, components path=@/components). 3. Install additional deps: `npm install clsx tailwind-merge lucide-react class-variance-authority`. 4. Create `/app/layout.tsx`: export metadata `{ title: 'TaxBridge - US-Canada Cross-Border Tax Calculator', description: 'Simplify cross-border tax filing for H-1B/TN workers', viewport: 'width=device-width, initial-scale=1' }`, import Inter from 'next/font/google' with subsets=['latin'], RootLayout component wrapping children in `<html lang="en" className="dark"><body className={inter.className}>{children}</body></html>`. 5. Update `/app/globals.css`: add shadcn CSS variables for dark mode (--background: 222.2 84% 4.9% for slate-900, --primary: 160 84.1% 39.4% for emerald-500, --secondary: 45 93.4% 47.5% for amber-500, --accent: 217.2 91.2% 59.8%), add custom utilities `.fintech-gradient { background: linear-gradient(135deg, hsl(var(--primary)), hsl(var(--secondary))); }`, `.glass-card { backdrop-filter: blur(16px); background: rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.6); border: 1px solid rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.1); }`. 6. Create `/app/page.tsx`: export default function HomePage() with three sections: (a) Hero: h1 'Simplify Your Cross-Border Tax Filing', subtitle 'Calculate US & Canada taxes on RSUs with Foreign Tax Credit optimization', CTA Button (shadcn) linking to /dashboard; (b) Features grid (3 cards): 'RSU Calculator' with Calculator icon, 'Tax Optimizer' with TrendingUp icon, 'Forms Checklist' with FileText icon (all from lucide-react), use shadcn Card components with glass-card class; (c) responsive layout using Tailwind grid (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6). 7. Install shadcn components: `npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card`. Acceptance: (1) `npm run dev` serves on localhost:3000, (2) landing page renders with dark slate background, emerald/amber accents, (3) responsive grid collapses to single column on mobile, (4) no TypeScript errors (`npm run build` succeeds), (5) ESLint passes (`npm run lint`), (6) all shadcn components render correctly.

title: Implement SQLite database layer with better-sqlite3, DDL schema, migrations, and type-safe CRUD helpers
description: 1. Install deps: `npm install better-sqlite3 && npm install -D @types/better-sqlite3 tsx`. 2. Create directory structure: `/lib/db/`, `/data/`, `/scripts/`. 3. Create `/lib/db/schema.sql` with DDL: (a) `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_profiles (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, name TEXT NOT NULL, province TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(province IN ('BC','ON','AB','QC')), state TEXT CHECK(state IN ('WA','CA','NY','TX','NONE') OR state IS NULL), filing_status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(filing_status IN ('Single','MarriedFilingJointly','MarriedFilingSeparately','HeadOfHousehold')), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);` (b) `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS rsu_entries (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, vest_date TEXT NOT NULL, fmv_usd REAL NOT NULL CHECK(fmv_usd > 0), shares INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK(shares > 0), employer TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(employer IN ('Meta','Amazon','Google','Microsoft')), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user_profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE);` (c) `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tax_calculations (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, rsu_entry_id INTEGER NOT NULL, us_federal_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(us_federal_tax >= 0), us_state_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(us_state_tax >= 0), ca_federal_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(ca_federal_tax >= 0), ca_provincial_tax REAL NOT NULL CHECK(ca_provincial_tax >= 0), ftc_amount REAL NOT NULL CHECK(ftc_amount >= 0), created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (rsu_entry_id) REFERENCES rsu_entries(id) ON DELETE CASCADE);` (d) Add indexes: `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_rsu_user ON rsu_entries(user_id);`, `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_rsu_vest_date ON rsu_entries(vest_date);`, `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tax_rsu ON tax_calculations(rsu_entry_id);`. 4. Create `/lib/db/index.ts`: (a) `let db: Database | null = null;` singleton, (b) `export function getDatabase(): Database { if (!db) { db = new Database(path.join(process.cwd(), 'data', 'taxbridge.db')); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'); } return db; }`, (c) `export function initializeDatabase(): void { const db = getDatabase(); const schema = fs.readFileSync(path.join(process.cwd(), 'lib', 'db', 'schema.sql'), 'utf-8'); db.exec(schema); }`, (d) `export const insertRSUEntry = (data: Omit<RSUEntry, 'id' | 'createdAt'>) => getDatabase().prepare('INSERT INTO rsu_entries (user_id, vest_date, fmv_usd, shares, employer) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(data.userId, data.vestDate, data.fmvUsd, data.shares, data.employer);`, (e) `export const getRSUEntriesByUser = (userId: number): RSUEntry[] => getDatabase().prepare('SELECT * FROM rsu_entries WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY vest_date DESC').all(userId) as RSUEntry[];`, (f) `export const insertTaxCalculation = (data: Omit<TaxCalculation, 'id' | 'createdAt'>) => getDatabase().prepare('INSERT INTO tax_calculations (rsu_entry_id, us_federal_tax, us_state_tax, ca_federal_tax, ca_provincial_tax, ftc_amount) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)').run(data.rsuEntryId, data.usFederalTax, data.usStateTax, data.caFederalTax, data.caProvincialTax, data.ftcAmount);`. 5. Create `/lib/db/seed.ts`: `import { getDatabase, insertRSUEntry } from './index'; const seedData = () => { const entries = [{ userId: 1, vestDate: '2025-02-15', fmvUsd: 485.32, shares: 50, employer: 'Meta' }, { userId: 1, vestDate: '2025-05-15', fmvUsd: 502.18, shares: 50, employer: 'Meta' }, { userId: 1, vestDate: '2025-08-15', fmvUsd: 478.90, shares: 50, employer: 'Amazon' }]; entries.forEach(e => insertRSUEntry(e)); console.log('Seeded', entries.length, 'RSU entries'); }; seedData();`. 6. Create `/scripts/init-db.ts`: `import { initializeDatabase } from '../lib/db'; initializeDatabase(); console.log('Database initialized at /data/taxbridge.db');`. 7. Add npm scripts to package.json: `"db:init": "tsx scripts/init-db.ts", "db:seed": "tsx lib/db/seed.ts"`. 8. Add to `.gitignore`: `/data/*.db`, `/data/*.db-shm`, `/data/*.db-wal`. 9. Create `/data/.gitkeep` (empty file). Acceptance: (1) Run `npm run db:init` creates `/data/taxbridge.db` with WAL mode and foreign keys enabled, (2) `sqlite3 data/taxbridge.db '.schema'` shows all tables and indexes, (3) Run `npm run db:seed` inserts 3 rows, (4) `sqlite3 data/taxbridge.db 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM rsu_entries;'` returns 3, (5) TypeScript compiles with no errors, (6) CHECK constraints work (trying to insert negative fmv_usd throws error).

title: Define TypeScript domain types (RSUEntry, TaxCalculation, UserProfile) and 2025 IRS/CRA tax bracket constants with JSDoc sources
description: 1. Create `/lib/types.ts`: (a) `export enum Employer { Meta = 'Meta', Amazon = 'Amazon', Google = 'Google', Microsoft = 'Microsoft' }`, (b) `export enum Province { BC = 'BC', ON = 'ON', AB = 'AB', QC = 'QC' }`, (c) `export enum State { WA = 'WA', CA = 'CA', NY = 'NY', TX = 'TX', NONE = 'NONE' }`, (d) `export enum FilingStatus { Single = 'Single', MarriedFilingJointly = 'MarriedFilingJointly', MarriedFilingSeparately = 'MarriedFilingSeparately', HeadOfHousehold = 'HeadOfHousehold' }`, (e) `export interface RSUEntry { id?: number; userId: number; vestDate: string; fmvUsd: number; shares: number; employer: Employer; createdAt?: string; }`, (f) `export interface TaxCalculation { id?: number; rsuEntryId: number; usFederalTax: number; usStateTax: number; caFederalTax: number; caProvincialTax: number; ftcAmount: number; createdAt?: string; }`, (g) `export interface UserProfile { id?: number; name: string; province: Province; state: State | null; filingStatus: FilingStatus; createdAt?: string; }`, (h) `export interface TaxBracket { min: number; max: number | null; rate: number; }`. 2. Create `/lib/constants/tax-brackets.ts`: (a) `/** @see https://www.irs.gov/pub/irs-drop/rp-24-40.pdf IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40 */ export const US_FEDERAL_2025: Record<FilingStatus, TaxBracket[]> = { Single: [{ min: 0, max: 11925, rate: 0.10 }, { min: 11925, max: 48475, rate: 0.12 }, { min: 48475, max: 103350, rate: 0.22 }, { min: 103350, max: 197300, rate: 0.24 }, { min: 197300, max: 250525, rate: 0.32 }, { min: 250525, max: 626350, rate: 0.35 }, { min: 626350, max: null, rate: 0.37 }], MarriedFilingJointly: [{ min: 0, max: 23850, rate: 0.10 }, { min: 23850, max: 96950, rate: 0.12 }, { min: 96950, max: 206700, rate: 0.22 }, { min: 206700, max: 394600, rate: 0.24 }, { min: 394600, max: 501050, rate: 0.32 }, { min: 501050, max: 751600, rate: 0.35 }, { min: 751600, max: null, rate: 0.37 }] /* other filing statuses omitted for brevity */ };`, (b) `/** @see https://www.ftb.ca.gov/forms/2024/2024-540-tax-rate-schedules.pdf */ export const US_STATE_2025: Record<State, { brackets: TaxBracket[] }> = { WA: { brackets: [] }, // No state income tax TX: { brackets: [] }, // No state income tax CA: { brackets: [{ min: 0, max: 10412, rate: 0.01 }, { min: 10412, max: 24684, rate: 0.02 }, { min: 24684, max: 38959, rate: 0.04 }, { min: 38959, max: 54081, rate: 0.06 }, { min: 54081, max: 68350, rate: 0.08 }, { min: 68350, max: 349137, rate: 0.093 }, { min: 349137, max: 418961, rate: 0.103 }, { min: 418961, max: 698271, rate: 0.113 }, { min: 698271, max: null, rate: 0.123 }] }, NY: { brackets: [{ min: 0, max: 8500, rate: 0.04 }, { min: 8500, max: 11700, rate: 0.045 }, { min: 11700, max: 13900, rate: 0.0525 }, { min: 13900, max: 80650, rate: 0.055 }, { min: 80650, max: 215400, rate: 0.06 }, { min: 215400, max: 1077550, rate: 0.0685 }, { min: 1077550, max: 5000000, rate: 0.0965 }, { min: 5000000, max: 25000000, rate: 0.103 }, { min: 25000000, max: null, rate: 0.109 }] }, NONE: { brackets: [] } };`, (c) `/** @see https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/tax/individuals/frequently-asked-questions-individuals/canadian-income-tax-rates-individuals-current-previous-years.html */ export const CA_FEDERAL_2025: TaxBracket[] = [{ min: 0, max: 55867, rate: 0.15 }, { min: 55867, max: 111733, rate: 0.205 }, { min: 111733, max: 173205, rate: 0.26 }, { min: 173205, max: 246752, rate: 0.29 }, { min: 246752, max: null, rate: 0.33 }];`, (d) `/** @see https://www2.gov.bc.ca/gov/content/taxes/income-taxes/personal/tax-rates and https://www.ontario.ca/document/personal-income-tax-rates */ export const CA_PROVINCIAL_2025: Record<Province, TaxBracket[]> = { BC: [{ min: 0, max: 47937, rate: 0.0506 }, { min: 47937, max: 95875, rate: 0.077 }, { min: 95875, max: 110076, rate: 0.105 }, { min: 110076, max: 133664, rate: 0.1229 }, { min: 133664, max: 181232, rate: 0.147 }, { min: 181232, max: 252752, rate: 0.168 }, { min: 252752, max: null, rate: 0.205 }], ON: [{ min: 0, max: 51446, rate: 0.0505 }, { min: 51446, max: 102894, rate: 0.0915 }, { min: 102894, max: 150000, rate: 0.1116 }, { min: 150000, max: 220000, rate: 0.1216 }, { min: 220000, max: null, rate: 0.1316 }] /* AB, QC omitted for brevity */ };`, (e) `export const STANDARD_DEDUCTION_2025: Record<FilingStatus, number> = { Single: 15000, MarriedFilingJointly: 30000, MarriedFilingSeparately: 15000, HeadOfHousehold: 22500 };`, (f) `/** @see https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/tax/individuals/topics/about-your-tax-return/tax-return/completing-a-tax-return/deductions-credits-expenses/line-30000-basic-personal-amount.html */ export const BASIC_PERSONAL_AMOUNT_2025 = 15705;`. 3. Create `/lib/utils/currency.ts`: `/** Convert USD to CAD using Bank of Canada exchange rate. @param amountUsd - Amount in US dollars @param rate - Exchange rate (CAD per USD, e.g. 1.35) @returns Amount in Canadian dollars, rounded to 2 decimals */ export function convertUsdToCad(amountUsd: number, rate: number): number { return Math.round(amountUsd * rate * 100) / 100; }`. 4. Create `/lib/utils/tax-calculator.ts` stub: `import type { TaxBracket } from '../types'; /** Calculate tax using marginal bracket system. @param income - Taxable income @param brackets - Tax bracket array @returns Total tax owed */ export function calculateTax(income: number, brackets: TaxBracket[]): number { let tax = 0; for (const bracket of brackets) { if (income <= bracket.min) break; const taxableInBracket = bracket.max ? Math.min(income, bracket.max) - bracket.min : income - bracket.min; tax += taxableInBracket * bracket.rate; } return Math.round(tax * 100) / 100; }`. Acceptance: (1) `npm run build` succeeds with no TypeScript errors, (2) `npm run lint` passes, (3) Spot-check tax calculation: `calculateTax(100000, US_FEDERAL_2025.Single)` returns 17400 (10% on first $11,925 + 12% on next $36,550 + 22% on next $51,825 = $1,192.50 + $4,386 + $11,401.50), (4) All enum values match DB CHECK constraints from Task 2, (5) JSDoc comments include source URLs for IRS Rev. Proc. 2024-40, CRA tax tables, BC/ON provincial rates, (6) `convertUsdToCad(100, 1.35)` returns 135.00.

title: Implement Dashboard Server Component with RSU Data Grid and Aggregated Tax Stats
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/app/dashboard/page.tsx` - Next.js 15 Server Component with async data fetching
- `/components/dashboard/stats-card.tsx` - Type-safe stat card: `type StatCardProps = { title: string; value: string; subtitle?: string; trend?: 'up' | 'down'; icon: LucideIcon }`
- `/components/dashboard/rsu-list.tsx` - Client Component with type `'use client'; type SortableRSUListProps = { initialData: RsuEvent[] }` using useState for sort state
- `/components/dashboard/quick-actions.tsx` - Navigation links using Next.js Link component

**Dependencies:** None new (uses existing lucide-react, better-sqlite3)

**Implementation details:**
1. Create `/app/dashboard/page.tsx` as async Server Component:
   ```typescript
   import { rsuEventQueries } from '@/lib/queries';
   export default async function DashboardPage() {
     const userId = 1; // MVP hardcoded
     const rsuEvents = rsuEventQueries.findByUserId(userId);
     const totalIncome = rsuEvents.reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.total_value_usd, 0);
     return <DashboardLayout stats={...} rsuData={rsuEvents} />;
   }
   ```
2. Implement `/components/dashboard/rsu-list.tsx` with client-side sorting:
   - State: `const [sortConfig, setSortConfig] = useState<{key: keyof RsuEvent; direction: 'asc'|'desc'}>({ key: 'vesting_date', direction: 'desc' })`
   - Sort function: `const sorted = [...data].sort((a,b) => { ... })`
   - Column headers: clickable with sort indicators (ChevronUp/ChevronDown icons)
   - Columns: Vesting Date (formatted as 'MMM DD, YYYY'), Employer, Shares (formatted with commas), FMV (currency), Total Value (currency, bold)
3. Build `/components/dashboard/stats-card.tsx` with:
   - Card container: `className='bg-white rounded-lg shadow-sm p-6 border border-gray-200'`
   - Value display: `className='text-3xl font-bold text-gray-900'`
   - Icon: top-right corner with gradient background
4. Layout structure in page.tsx:
   - Grid: `className='grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6 mb-8'`
   - RSU table: full-width below stats, `className='overflow-x-auto'` for mobile
5. Placeholder stats calculation:
   - Total RSU Income YTD: sum of `total_value_usd`
   - US Tax: `totalIncome * 0.24` (placeholder 24% bracket)
   - Canada Tax: `totalIncome * 0.26` (placeholder 26% bracket)
   - Filing Status: check `form_completion` table progress (0-33%='Not Started', 34-66%='In Progress', 67-100%='Ready to File')

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Dashboard renders without hydration errors (Server Component pattern correct)
- All 4 stat cards display with proper formatting: Total RSU Income ($124,567.89), US Tax Est. ($29,896.29), Canada Tax Est. ($32,387.65), Filing Status badge
- RSU table shows test data sorted by vesting_date DESC by default
- Clicking column headers toggles sort direction (visual indicator updates)
- Mobile responsive: cards stack vertically on <768px, table scrolls horizontally
- Quick actions navigate: 'Add RSU Entry' → `/rsu-entry`, 'View Tax Summary' → `/calculator`
- No console errors or warnings in dev mode
result: The dashboard is now fully functional with all the requested features plus these additional action buttons. The server is running and ready for development at `http://localhost:3000/dashboard`.  Is there anything else you'd like me to implement or modify for the dashboard? [2026-03-18T06:28:21.547Z] Agent finished with exit code 0

title: [PROJECT] Foreign Tax Credit Optimizer
description: Implement FTC calculation to prevent double taxation and show optimal credit strategy

title: Implement Core FTC Calculation Engine (lib/tax/ftc-calculator.ts)
description:
**Files to create:**
- `/lib/tax/ftc-calculator.ts` (210 lines estimated)
- `/lib/tax/__tests__/ftc-calculator.test.ts` (150 lines estimated)

**Dependencies:**
```bash
npm install --save-dev vitest @vitest/ui
```

**Implementation:**

1. **Function signature** (export from ftc-calculator.ts):
```typescript
export interface FTCInput {
  grossIncome: number; // Total RSU income USD
  usFederalTax: number;
  usStateTax: number;
  canadaFederalTax: number;
  canadaProvincialTax: number;
  state: string; // e.g., 'WA', 'CA'
  province: string; // e.g., 'BC', 'ON'
  workDaysInCanada: number; // For Article XV allocation
  totalWorkDays: number;
}

export interface FTCResult {
  usFTC: number; // Foreign tax credit on Form 1116
  canadaFTC: number; // Foreign tax credit on T2209
  optimalStrategy: 'file-us-first' | 'file-canada-first';
  totalTaxWithFTC: number;
  totalTaxWithoutFTC: number;
  savings: number;
  details: {
    foreignIncomeRatio: number; // For Article XV
    usCreditable: number; // Amount creditable in US
    canadaCreditable: number; // Amount creditable in Canada
    excessFTC: number; // Carryforward amount if any
  };
}

export function calculateFTC(input: FTCInput): FTCResult
```

2. **IRS Form 1116 logic** (lines 20-80):
   - Calculate foreign income ratio: `workDaysInCanada / totalWorkDays` (Article XV allocation)
   - Foreign source income: `grossIncome * foreignIncomeRatio`
   - US tax on foreign income: `(usFederalTax + usStateTax) * foreignIncomeRatio`
   - Canadian tax paid: `canadaFederalTax + canadaProvincialTax`
   - **US FTC formula**: `Math.min(canadianTaxPaid, usTaxOnForeignIncome)`

3. **Canada T2209 logic** (lines 85-130):
   - Foreign income for Canadian purposes: `grossIncome * (1 - foreignIncomeRatio)` (US-earned portion)
   - US tax paid: `usFederalTax + usStateTax`
   - Federal FTC limit: `(foreignIncome / grossIncome) * canadaFederalTax`
   - Provincial FTC limit: `(foreignIncome / grossIncome) * canadaProvincialTax`
   - **Canada FTC formula**: `Math.min(usTaxPaid, federalLimit + provincialLimit)`

4. **Optimization strategy** (lines 135-180):
   - Scenario A (file US first): `totalTax = usFederalTax + usStateTax + canadaFederalTax + canadaProvincialTax - usFTC`
   - Scenario B (file Canada first): `totalTax = usFederalTax + usStateTax + canadaFederalTax + canadaProvincialTax - canadaFTC`
   - Return scenario with lower total
   - Calculate excess FTC (if foreign tax > limit, can carry forward 10 years US, 3 years Canada)

5. **Test cases** (ftc-calculator.test.ts):
   - Test 1: `$50k RSU, WA + BC, 260/260 days Canada` → Expect ~$7,500 US FTC, ~$0 Canada FTC (no state tax)
   - Test 2: `$200k RSU, CA + ON, 260/260 days Canada` → Expect ~$35,000 US FTC, ~$0 Canada FTC
   - Test 3: `$100k RSU, NY + BC, 130/260 days split` → Expect ~$8,500 US FTC, ~$4,200 Canada FTC (50/50 split)
   - Test 4: `$30k RSU, WA + BC, 260/260` → Verify no negative FTC
   - Test 5: High CA tax scenario with excess FTC → Verify carryforward calculation
   - Use `vitest` with `describe/it/expect` blocks
   - Tolerance: `expect(result.usFTC).toBeCloseTo(expected, 0)` (±$1)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] All 5 test cases pass with `npm run test`
- [ ] Function returns optimal strategy matching manual calculation
- [ ] JSDoc comments explain Article XV sourcing rules
- [ ] No runtime errors with edge inputs (zero income, negative tax)
- [ ] Type safety: all inputs/outputs strongly typed with TypeScript strict mode

title: Implement Clerk Authentication with Multi-Tenant Data Isolation
description:
**Dependencies:** `npm install @clerk/nextjs @clerk/themes`

**Environment Variables (.env.local):**
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=pk_test_...
CLERK_SECRET_KEY=sk_test_...
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/sign-in
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL=/sign-up
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_IN_URL=/onboarding
NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_AFTER_SIGN_UP_URL=/onboarding
CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_...
```

**Files to Create:**
1. `/app/layout.tsx` — Wrap children with `<ClerkProvider>`
2. `/middleware.ts` — Export Clerk middleware protecting routes:
```ts
import { authMiddleware } from '@clerk/nextjs';
export default authMiddleware({
  publicRoutes: ['/', '/pricing', '/api/stripe/webhook'],
  ignoredRoutes: ['/api/auth/webhook']
});
export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!.*\\..*|_next).*)', '/', '/(api|trpc)(.*)'] };
```
3. `/app/api/auth/webhook/route.ts` — Clerk webhook handler for user.created/user.updated events, creates/updates user_profiles record
4. `/app/(auth)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx` — `<SignIn />` component
5. `/app/(auth)/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx` — `<SignUp />` component
6. `/app/onboarding/page.tsx` — Form collecting province (select BC/ON/QC/AB), state (select CA/WA/NY/TX), filing_status (single/married), saves to user_profiles, redirects to /dashboard
7. `/components/Header.tsx` — Add `<UserButton afterSignOutUrl='/' />` to header

**Database Migration (`/lib/db/migrations/003_user_profiles.sql`):**
```sql
CREATE TABLE user_profiles (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  clerk_user_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  subscription_tier TEXT DEFAULT 'free' CHECK(subscription_tier IN ('free', 'pro', 'enterprise')),
  stripe_customer_id TEXT,
  trial_ends_at INTEGER,
  province TEXT,
  state TEXT,
  filing_status TEXT,
  created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
  updated_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch())
);
CREATE INDEX idx_clerk_user_id ON user_profiles(clerk_user_id);

ALTER TABLE rsu_entries ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES user_profiles(id);
CREATE INDEX idx_rsu_entries_user_id ON rsu_entries(user_id);
```

**Database Helper (`/lib/db/index.ts`):**
```ts
export function getUserProfile(clerkUserId: string) {
  return db.prepare('SELECT * FROM user_profiles WHERE clerk_user_id = ?').get(clerkUserId);
}
export function createUserProfile(clerkUserId: string, data: {...}) {
  return db.prepare('INSERT INTO user_profiles (clerk_user_id, ...) VALUES (?, ...)').run(clerkUserId, ...);
}
```

**Modify All Queries:**
- `/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — Get `userId` from `auth()`, fetch user_profile, pass to RSU queries
- `/app/rsu-entry/page.tsx` — Insert with `user_id` from user_profiles.id
- `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx` — Filter by user_id

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] User signs up with email/password or Google OAuth
- [ ] Webhook creates user_profiles record with clerk_user_id
- [ ] User redirected to /onboarding, fills province/state/filing_status
- [ ] User lands on /dashboard showing only their RSU entries
- [ ] User A cannot see User B's data (verify with 2 test accounts)
- [ ] Unauthenticated users redirected to /sign-in when accessing /dashboard
- [ ] <UserButton /> in header allows sign out

title: Implement Stripe Checkout & Webhook-Driven Subscription Management
Build Admin Analytics Dashboard with Event Tracking & Conversion Metrics
description:
**Dependencies:** `npm install recharts date-fns` **Files to Create:** 1. `/lib/db/migrations/004_analytics.sql`: ```sql CREATE TABLE analytics_events ( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES user_profiles(id), event_name TEXT NOT NULL, metadata TEXT, -- JSON string created_at INTEGER DEFAULT (unixepoch()) ); CREATE INDEX idx_events_user_created ON analytics_events(user_id, created_at); CREATE INDEX idx_events_name_created ON analytics_events(event_name, created_at); ``` 2. `/lib/analytics.ts`: ```ts import { db } from './db'; export function trackEvent(userId: number, eventName: string, metadata?: Record) { db.prepare('INSERT INTO analytics_events (user_id, event_name, metadata) VALUES (?, ?, ?)') .run(userId, eventName, metadata ? JSON.stringify(metadata) : null); } export type AnalyticsEvent = 'user_signed_up' | 'profile_completed' | 'rsu_entry_created' | 'tax_calculation_viewed' | 'ftc_optimizer_used' | 'pdf_exported' | 'forms_checklist_opened' | 'upgraded_to_pro' | 'upgraded_to_enterprise'; ``` 3. **Add Tracking Calls:** - `/app/api/auth/webhook/route.ts` — After user creation: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'user_signed_up')` - `/app/onboarding/page.tsx` — After form submit: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'profile_completed', { province, state })` - `/app/rsu-entry/page.tsx` — After RSU insert: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'rsu_entry_created', { employer, shares })` - `/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — On load: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'tax_calculation_viewed')` - FTC Optimizer component — On optimize click: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'ftc_optimizer_used')` - PDF export handler — On export: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'pdf_exported')` - `/app/forms-checklist/page.tsx` — On load: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'forms_checklist_opened')` - `/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts` — After tier update: `trackEvent(userProfile.id, 'upgraded_to_pro')` 4. `/app/admin/analytics/page.tsx` — Admin dashboard (protected by Clerk publicMetadata.role === 'admin'): ```tsx import { auth } from '@clerk/nextjs'; import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'; import { BarChart, LineChart } from 'recharts'; export default async function AnalyticsPage() { const { userId } = auth(); const user = await clerkClient.users.getUser(userId); if (user.publicMetadata.role !== 'admin') redirect('/dashboard'); const dau = db.prepare(` SELECT DATE(created_at, 'unixepoch') as date, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) as count FROM analytics_events WHERE created_at >= unixepoch('now', '-30 days') GROUP BY date `).all(); const conversionFunnel = { signups: db.prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM analytics_events WHERE event_name = 'user_signed_up'").pluck().get(), profileCompleted: db.prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM analytics_events WHERE event_name = 'profile_completed'").pluck().get(), firstRSU: db.prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM analytics_events WHERE event_name = 'rsu_entry_created'").pluck().get(), upgradedToPro: db.prepare("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM analytics_events WHERE event_name = 'upgraded_to_pro'").pluck().get() }; const featureUsage = db.prepare(` SELECT event_name, COUNT(*) as count FROM analytics_events WHERE event_name IN ('tax_calculation_viewed', 'ftc_optimizer_used', 'pdf_exported', 'forms_checklist_opened') GROUP BY event_name `).all(); const mrr = db.prepare(` SELECT subscription_tier, COUNT(*) * CASE WHEN subscription_tier = 'pro' THEN 299 WHEN subscription_tier = 'enterprise' THEN 2000 ELSE 0 END / 12 as monthly_revenue FROM user_profiles WHERE subscription_tier != 'free' GROUP BY subscription_tier `).all(); return (

Analytics Dashboard

Daily Active Users (30d) Conversion Funnel Signups: {conversionFunnel.signups} → Profile: {conversionFunnel.profileCompleted} ({(conversionFunnel.profileCompleted / conversionFunnel.signups * 100).toFixed(1)}%) → First RSU: {conversionFunnel.firstRSU} → Pro: {conversionFunnel.upgradedToPro} ({(conversionFunnel.upgradedToPro / conversionFunnel.signups * 100).toFixed(1)}% overall conversion) Feature Usage MRR${mrr.reduce((sum, tier) => sum + tier.monthly_revenue, 0).toFixed(2)}/mo
); } ``` 5. **Grant Admin Role (Manual via Clerk Dashboard):** - Go to Users → Select admin user → Metadata → Public Metadata → Add `{ "role": "admin" }` **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Analytics events table populated with user actions (verify in SQLite viewer) - [ ] Admin user can access /admin/analytics (non-admin gets 404) - [ ] DAU chart shows daily user activity over 30 days - [ ] Conversion funnel calculates: Signups → Profile → First RSU → Upgraded to Pro with percentages - [ ] Feature usage heatmap shows counts for tax_calculation_viewed, ftc_optimizer_used, pdf_exported, forms_checklist_opened - [ ] MRR chart displays monthly recurring revenue (e.g., 3 Pro users × $299/12 = $74.75/mo) - [ ] Conversion rate from free to Pro is >= 5% (use test data to validate calculation logic)

title: [PROJECT] SEO & Content-Driven User Acquisition
description: Build organic traffic through SEO-optimized landing pages and educational content targeting 'cross border tax H1B' searches (12K monthly searches)

title: Build SEO-Optimized Landing Pages with Structured Data and Analytics
description:
FILES TO CREATE:
- app/(marketing)/us-canada-tax-calculator/page.tsx - Interactive calculator component with RSU input fields, real-time tax calculation (reuse logic from app/ftc-optimizer/ftc-calculator.ts), email capture form, CTA to /signup
- app/(marketing)/h1b-rsu-tax-guide/page.tsx - 2000-word MDX content with table of contents, Article XV explainer, FTC walkthrough, common mistakes section, FAQ accordion
- app/(marketing)/canada-tax-filing-checklist/page.tsx - Interactive checklist with checkboxes (client-side state only), deadline calendar, CRA form links
- app/(marketing)/layout.tsx - Shared marketing layout without auth checks
- lib/seo/metadata.ts - Helper functions for generateMetadata() with OpenGraph/Twitter card defaults
- lib/seo/structured-data.ts - JSON-LD generators for Article, FAQPage, WebApplication schemas
- public/sitemap.xml - Static sitemap with all landing pages, priority 0.8
- public/robots.txt - Allow all, sitemap reference

DEPENDENCIES:
npm install @next/third-parties next-seo schema-dts

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Create (marketing) route group to isolate marketing pages from app layout
2. Each page.tsx exports async generateMetadata() with keyword-optimized title (60 chars), description (155 chars), OpenGraph image (1200x630)
3. Embed JSON-LD via <script type="application/ld+json"> in component, using schema-dts for type safety
4. Calculator page: Client component with useState for inputs, displays dual tax comparison table, email form POSTs to /api/marketing/capture-lead
5. Guide page: Server component with static MDX content, table of contents with anchor links, related articles sidebar
6. Checklist page: Client component with FAQPage schema, each checklist item is a FAQ entry
7. Add internal links: calculator -> guide ("Learn more about Article XV"), guide -> calculator ("Try the calculator"), both -> /pricing
8. Create app/api/marketing/capture-lead/route.ts to store emails in leads table (id, email, source_page, created_at)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- npm run build succeeds with static page generation for all 3 routes
- curl -I https://taxbridge.app/us-canada-tax-calculator returns 200 with meta tags in HTML source
- Google Search Console shows pages indexed within 48h of sitemap submission
- PageSpeed Insights scores: Performance >90, Accessibility 100, Best Practices 100, SEO 100
- Rich results test (search.google.com/test/rich-results) validates JSON-LD for all pages
- Social share preview (opengraph.xyz) renders correct image and description

title: Implement SendGrid Email Drip Campaign with Vercel Cron
description:
FILES TO CREATE:
- app/api/cron/email-drip/route.ts - Vercel Cron handler (export config with cron: '0 9 * * *' for daily 9am UTC)
- lib/email/sendgrid.ts - SendGrid client wrapper with sendEmail(to, templateId, dynamicData) function
- lib/email/templates.ts - Email template content (HTML strings or SendGrid template IDs)
- lib/db/queries/drip-campaign.ts - getUsersForDripEmail(dayOffset: number) query
- app/unsubscribe/page.tsx - Unsubscribe form with email input, updates user_profiles.email_preferences JSON field
- app/api/unsubscribe/route.ts - POST handler to set {marketing_emails: false} in user_profiles.email_preferences

DATABASE MIGRATIONS:
ALTER TABLE user_profiles ADD COLUMN email_preferences TEXT DEFAULT '{"marketing_emails": true}';
CREATE TABLE email_events (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  event_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'drip_welcome', 'drip_day3', 'drip_day7', 'drip_day14'
  sent_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  opened_at TEXT,
  clicked_at TEXT,
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user_profiles(id)
);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_email_events_user_type ON email_events(user_id, event_type);

DEPENDENCIES:
npm install @sendgrid/mail

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Set SENDGRID_API_KEY in .env.local and Vercel environment variables
2. Create SendGrid Dynamic Templates in SendGrid dashboard for each email (welcome, day3, day7, day14), note template IDs
3. Cron route logic:
   - Query user_profiles where subscription_tier='free' AND created_at matches trigger (DATE(created_at) = DATE('now', '-N days'))
   - LEFT JOIN email_events to exclude users who already received that email type
   - Filter where email_preferences->>'marketing_emails' = 'true'
   - For each user, send email via SendGrid, insert row into email_events with event_type and sent_at
4. Unsubscribe page: Form with email input, on submit calls /api/unsubscribe, shows confirmation message
5. All email templates include unsubscribe link: https://taxbridge.app/unsubscribe?email={{email}}
6. Day 14 email includes discount code SAVE20 valid for 7 days

VERCEL CRON SETUP:
Add to vercel.json:
{
  "crons": [{
    "path": "/api/cron/email-drip",
    "schedule": "0 9 * * *"
  }]
}

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Create test user with created_at = DATE('now'), verify welcome email arrives within 1 hour (manual trigger: curl https://taxbridge.app/api/cron/email-drip -H 'Authorization: Bearer CRON_SECRET')
- Create test user with created_at = DATE('now', '-3 days'), verify day 3 email sent on next cron run
- Check email_events table has unique constraint preventing duplicate sends
- Unsubscribe flow: submit form, verify email_preferences updated, no further emails sent
- SendGrid dashboard shows open rate >25%, click rate >5% after 7 days of live traffic

title: Build Stripe-Integrated Affiliate Program with Partner Portal
description:
FILES TO CREATE:
- app/api/partners/signup/route.ts - POST handler for affiliate application (name, firm, email, commission_rate_requested)
- app/api/partners/approve/route.ts - POST handler for admin approval (requires admin auth), generates unique referral_code
- app/partners/page.tsx - Public affiliate signup form with firm details, commission proposal (default 20%)
- app/partners/dashboard/[code]/page.tsx - Partner dashboard showing referrals, earnings, unique link, with auth check (code must match session user)
- app/admin/partners/page.tsx - Admin table of pending/approved partners with approve/reject buttons, requires isAdmin check
- lib/db/queries/affiliates.ts - CRUD functions for affiliate_partners table
- lib/stripe/affiliate-tracking.ts - trackAffiliateReferral(sessionId, referralCode) function

DATABASE MIGRATIONS:
CREATE TABLE affiliate_partners (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  partner_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  firm_name TEXT NOT NULL,
  email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  referral_code TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  commission_rate REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.20,
  status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- 'pending', 'approved', 'rejected'
  total_referrals INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  total_revenue REAL DEFAULT 0.0,
  stripe_connect_id TEXT,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  approved_at TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE affiliate_referrals (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  affiliate_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  subscription_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  commission_amount REAL NOT NULL,
  commission_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending', -- 'pending', 'paid'
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (affiliate_id) REFERENCES affiliate_partners(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES user_profiles(id)
);

ALTER TABLE user_profiles ADD COLUMN referred_by TEXT; -- referral_code

DEPENDENCIES:
No new dependencies (use existing @stripe/stripe-js, stripe server SDK)

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Signup flow:
   - Partner fills form at /partners, POST to /api/partners/signup
   - Generate referral_code: nanoid(10).toUpperCase() (install nanoid)
   - Insert into affiliate_partners with status='pending'
   - Send notification email to admin and confirmation email to partner
2. Approval flow:
   - Admin visits /app/admin/partners, sees pending applications
   - Click Approve -> POST /api/partners/approve with partner_id
   - Update status='approved', approved_at=now()
   - Send approval email to partner with unique link: https://taxbridge.app?ref=CODE
3. Referral tracking:
   - Modify app/signup/page.tsx to capture ?ref= query param, store in localStorage
   - On Stripe checkout, pass referral_code as metadata: {referred_by: code}
   - In app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts, handle checkout.session.completed:
     - Extract referred_by from session.metadata
     - Query affiliate_partners WHERE referral_code = referred_by AND status='approved'
     - If found, calculate commission: session.amount_total * partner.commission_rate / 100
     - Insert affiliate_referrals row, update user_profiles.referred_by
     - Update affiliate_partners: total_referrals++, total_revenue += commission
4. Partner dashboard:
   - Auth check: session user email must match affiliate_partners.email for that code
   - Display: total_referrals, total_revenue, list of recent referrals (user masked as 'User #123', commission amount, date)
   - Unique link with copy button: https://taxbridge.app?ref={referral_code}
   - Payout section: 'Payouts processed monthly via Stripe Connect' (future: integrate Stripe Connect for automated payouts)
5. Admin dashboard:
   - Table with columns: firm_name, partner_name, email, commission_rate, status, created_at, actions (Approve/Reject)
   - Filter by status (pending/approved/rejected)

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Law firm submits application, receives confirmation email, sees 'pending' status
- Admin approves application, partner receives email with referral link https://taxbridge.app?ref=XYZ123
- User clicks referral link, signs up, subscribes to Pro ($299/year)
- Webhook fires, affiliate_referrals row created with commission $59.80 (20% of $299)
- Partner dashboard shows 1 referral, $59.80 earned
- Admin can view all partners, filter by status, see aggregate stats (total commissions owed)
- Edge case: duplicate referral codes return 409 error on signup

title: [PROJECT] Enterprise Features for Scale
description: Build B2B features enabling immigration firms and corporate tax departments to manage multiple clients, unlocking $2K/seat enterprise revenue

title: Multi-Client Organization Management with RBAC
description:
**Database Schema (migrations/003_enterprise_orgs.sql)**:
- CREATE TABLE organizations (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
- CREATE TABLE organization_members (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, org_id INTEGER REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, user_id INTEGER REFERENCES user_profiles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, role TEXT CHECK(role IN ('admin', 'member', 'client')) NOT NULL, invited_at TEXT, joined_at TEXT, UNIQUE(org_id, user_id))
- ALTER TABLE user_profiles ADD COLUMN org_id INTEGER REFERENCES organizations(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
- CREATE INDEX idx_org_members_org ON organization_members(org_id)
- CREATE INDEX idx_org_members_user ON organization_members(user_id)

**Files to Create**:
- `/app/enterprise/clients/page.tsx`: Server component fetching org members via getOrgClients(), renders ClientDashboard client component with table (columns: name, total_rsu_ytd, total_tax_owed, filing_status, last_activity), search input, filter dropdowns (province, state, employer), bulk action buttons
- `/app/enterprise/clients/ClientDashboard.tsx`: 'use client' component with react-table for sorting/filtering, bulk selection checkboxes, export to CSV button calling /api/enterprise/export
- `/app/api/enterprise/clients/route.ts`: GET handler with auth check (session.user.role === 'admin'), SQL query joins organization_members + user_profiles + rsu_entries (aggregated SUM), POST handler for client invitation sending email via nodemailer/resend with signup token
- `/app/api/enterprise/invite/[token]/route.ts`: Validates invite token, auto-associates user.org_id on signup completion
- `/components/OrgSwitcher.tsx`: Dropdown in header (visible if user.role === 'admin'), fetches user's orgs from /api/enterprise/orgs, updates session context on switch
- `/lib/db/queries/enterprise.ts`: getOrgClients(orgId, filters), inviteClient(orgId, email, role), switchOrg(userId, newOrgId)

**Dependencies**: No new deps needed (using existing better-sqlite3, next-auth, tailwindcss)

**Row-Level Security**: Wrap all DB queries with RLS middleware checking: if user.role !== 'admin' OR user.org_id !== query.org_id, throw 403. Apply in /lib/db/middleware.ts using higher-order function withOrgAccess(handler).

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- CPA creates org "Smith Tax LLP", invites client1@example.com
- Client receives email, signs up via token link, user_profiles.org_id auto-set
- CPA visits /enterprise/clients, sees client in table with YTD RSU $150K, tax owed $45K, status "In Progress"
- CPA filters by province=ON, exports CSV with 10 clients
- Non-admin user visiting /enterprise/clients gets 403
- Admin managing 2 orgs can switch via header dropdown, sees different client lists

title: CSV Bulk Import with Validation and Transaction Safety
description:
**Files to Create**:
- `/app/dashboard/import/page.tsx`: Server component wrapping ImportFlow client component
- `/app/dashboard/import/ImportFlow.tsx`: 'use client' component with 3-step wizard: (1) Upload zone using react-dropzone accepting .csv, (2) Preview table showing parsed rows + validation errors, (3) Confirmation success/error summary
- `/app/api/rsu/bulk/route.ts`: POST handler accepting { rows: Array<{vesting_date, employer, shares, fmv_usd, us_state, canada_province}> }, wraps in db.transaction(() => { rows.forEach(r => db.prepare(INSERT_RSU_SQL).run(r)) }), returns { success: number, errors: Array<{row, message}> }
- `/lib/validation/csv.ts`: validateCSVRow(row) => { date: z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/), employer: z.enum(['Meta', 'Amazon', 'Google', 'Microsoft', 'Apple']), shares: z.number().positive(), fmv_usd: z.number().positive(), us_state: z.enum([US_STATES]), canada_province: z.enum([CA_PROVINCES]) }, returns { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }
- `/public/templates/rsu_import_template.csv`: Sample CSV with headers and 3 example rows

**Dependencies to Install**:
- `npm install papaparse @types/papaparse react-dropzone zod`

**Implementation Approach**:
1. User drops CSV file → react-dropzone onDrop triggers Papa.parse(file, { header: true, skipEmptyLines: true })
2. Frontend loops parsed rows, calls validateCSVRow() from /lib/validation/csv.ts using Zod schemas
3. Display preview table: green rows (valid), red rows (invalid with error tooltip)
4. User clicks "Import" → POST to /api/rsu/bulk with validated rows only
5. API route uses db.transaction() for atomicity: if any INSERT fails, rollback entire batch
6. Return { imported: 95, failed: 5, errors: [{row: 23, message: "Invalid date format"}] }
7. Show success modal with counts, offer "Download Error Report" CSV if failures exist
8. Error CSV has original row data + "error_reason" column

**Edge Cases**:
- Max 1000 rows enforced in API (return 413 if exceeded)
- Duplicate detection: check if (user_id, vesting_date, employer) already exists, skip with warning
- Empty CSV: show friendly "No valid rows found" message
- Malformed CSV: Papa.parse errors caught, display "Invalid CSV format"

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- Enterprise user downloads /templates/rsu_import_template.csv, fills 100 rows with Meta/Google vesting data
- Uploads CSV via drag-drop, sees preview table with all 100 rows green (valid)
- Clicks Import, sees progress indicator, receives success message "100 entries imported"
- All 100 RSU entries appear in /dashboard with correct vesting dates, FMV, tax calculations
- Upload CSV with 10 invalid rows (bad dates, unknown employer): preview shows 90 green, 10 red with error tooltips
- Import only processes 90 valid rows, downloads error CSV with 10 failed rows + reasons
- Upload 1500-row CSV: API rejects with "Maximum 1000 rows allowed" error

title: [PROJECT] Product Differentiation & Retention Features
description: Build unique features competitors lack to increase customer lifetime value and reduce churn

title: Multi-Year Tax Dashboard with FTC Carryforward Engine
description: DATABASE SCHEMA: (1) Migrate rsu_entries table: `ALTER TABLE rsu_entries ADD COLUMN year INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2026; CREATE INDEX idx_rsu_entries_year ON rsu_entries(year);` (2) Create ftc_carryforward table: `CREATE TABLE ftc_carryforward (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, year INTEGER NOT NULL, unused_ftc_cad REAL NOT NULL, source_year INTEGER NOT NULL, expires_at INTEGER NOT NULL, applied_to_year INTEGER, created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); CREATE INDEX idx_ftc_year ON ftc_carryforward(year);`. BACKEND: (1) `/lib/db/migrations/003_multi_year.sql` - SQL migration file. (2) `/lib/ftc-carryforward.ts` - Export `calculateFTCCarryforward(year: number): Promise<{availableCarryforward: number, excessFTC: number, carryforwardRecords: Array}>` that queries ftc_carryforward for unused credits where `year >= sourceYear - 10 && year <= sourceYear + 10 && applied_to_year IS NULL`, sums unused_ftc_cad. Also export `applyCarryforward(currentYear: number, taxOwedCAD: number): Promise<{reducedTax: number, appliedAmount: number}>` that applies available carryforward and updates applied_to_year. (3) Modify existing FTC calculation in `/lib/tax-calculator.ts` to call `applyCarryforward()` after computing base FTC, return both original and carryforward-adjusted tax. FRONTEND: (1) `/app/dashboard/multi-year/page.tsx` - Server component that fetches RSU entries grouped by year (`SELECT year, SUM(fmv_usd * shares) as total_income FROM rsu_entries GROUP BY year ORDER BY year`), tax paid by year/country from existing calculations, available FTC carryforward. (2) Year selector: `<select>` dropdown 2022-2026, updates URL param `?year=2025`, re-fetches data. (3) Charts: Install `npm install recharts`. Use `<LineChart>` for income trend (x-axis: year, y-axis: total RSU income USD), `<AreaChart>` stacked for cumulative tax (two areas: US tax, Canada tax). (4) FTC carryforward banner: If `availableCarryforward > 0`, show `<Alert>Potential savings from {sourceYear} FTC carryforward: ${appliedAmount.toLocaleString()}</Alert>`. ACCEPTANCE: User with RSU entries spanning 2023-2025 navigates to /dashboard/multi-year, sees line chart showing income progression ($80K → $120K → $150K), stacked area chart showing cumulative tax, and banner 'Potential savings from 2023 FTC carryforward: $1,250' when excess US tax from 2023 ($3,500 unused FTC) applies to 2025 Canada tax liability.

title: Streaming AI Tax Advisor with Claude API Integration
description: DEPENDENCIES: `npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk ai` (Vercel AI SDK for streaming utilities). ENVIRONMENT: Add `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...` to `.env.local`. DATABASE: `CREATE TABLE tax_recommendations (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_context_hash TEXT NOT NULL, recommendations TEXT NOT NULL, feedback INTEGER CHECK(feedback IN (-1, 0, 1)) DEFAULT 0, created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP); CREATE INDEX idx_recommendations_feedback ON tax_recommendations(feedback);`. BACKEND: (1) `/app/api/ai/tax-advice/route.ts` - POST handler using `export const runtime = 'edge'`. Accept body: `{rsuEntries: Array<{year, vestingDate, fmvUSD, shares, employer}>, province: string, state: string, ftcResults: {usTaxUSD, canadaTaxCAD, ftcCAD}, filingStatus: string}`. (2) Initialize Anthropic client: `const anthropic = new Anthropic({apiKey: process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY})`. (3) Build prompt: `You are a cross-border tax optimization expert. Analyze this H-1B/TN worker's situation: [JSON context]. Provide exactly 3 specific, actionable tax optimization strategies. For each: (1) Strategy title, (2) Estimated annual savings in CAD, (3) Implementation steps, (4) Risks/caveats. Focus on: RSU vesting timing, provincial tax arbitrage, RRSP contributions (18% of prior year income up to $31,560 CAD deduction), state tax residency planning, FTC maximization. Format as markdown with ## headers.` (4) Stream response: `const stream = await anthropic.messages.stream({model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6', max_tokens: 2000, messages: [{role: 'user', content: prompt}]}); return new Response(stream.toReadableStream())`. (5) On completion, hash user context with SHA-256, insert into tax_recommendations. FRONTEND: (1) `/components/AITaxAdvisor.tsx` - Client component with 'Get AI Recommendations' button.
